steps.title: Use the table as points
steps.content: The table gives the points $(1,34)$, $(2,31)$, $(3,28)$, and $(4,25)$. Since $g$ is linear and the question asks for $b$ in $g(x)=mx+b$, this is a direct table-regression problem.

steps.title: Run the Desmos regression
steps.content: Enter the table values as $x_1=[1,2,3,4]$ and $y_1=[34,31,28,25]$, then type $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=-3$ and $b=37$.

steps.title: Confirm by hand
steps.content: The outputs decrease by $3$ each time $x$ increases by $1$, so $m=-3$. Using $(1,34)$ in $g(x)=mx+b$ gives $$34=-3(1)+b$$ so $b=37$. The answer is 37.
